reasoning: The Lean statement accurately captures the informal claim. `[CommRing R]` and `[Module.Projective R M]` match the hypotheses "commutative ring R" and "projective module M". The existential `∃ (N : Type) (_ : AddCommGroup N) (_ : Module R N), Module.Free R N` expresses "there exists a free R-module N". The conclusion `Module.Free R (N × M)` correctly expresses "M ⊕ N is free", since in Mathlib the binary direct sum of two modules is encoded as the product type `N × M` (confirmed by the `Module.Free.prod` instance in `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Basis/Prod.lean`). The order "N × M" vs. informal "M ⊕ N" is immaterial since direct sum is commutative. The universe restriction to `Type` (Type 0) is a standard formalization choice and does not change the mathematical content — this is the classical Eilenberg swindle.
